title: Estimating pupil numbers: how to complete your return for 2026 to 2027
body: "The estimating pupil numbers (EPN) form is now available for schools to provide estimated pupil numbers for the academic year 2026 to 2027, where the funding agreement allows for estimate funding.   Please submit your form on or before 5:00pm Friday 24 October 2025. Your final budgets may be delayed if we do not receive your pupil number estimates by the deadline.   For multi-academy trusts, a return is required for each estimate funded academy in your trust. Once logged into the form, you will be able to see details for all academies within your trust that are in scope of this process.  We appreciate that estimating pupil numbers is not an exact science and can prove challenging. For most schools, a reasonable starting point to determine your estimated pupil numbers for the academic year 2026 to 2027 is your current actual intake. Where schools are expecting new year groups to be occupied within academic year 2026 to 2027, these should be reflected in the return.  Where estimates of pupil numbers are not provided, we will use the most recent census data to calculate your funding. If you are a growing school that is still adding year groups, this can result in a funding shortfall.  Moderation of estimated pupil numbers  We will review all estimated pupil numbers to ensure they are reasonable and well-evidenced. We will consider the October 2025 school census return, trends in pupil numbers, retention rates, the accuracy of previous estimates alongside any information you provide. If we have not accepted your figures, we will explain why, and schools will be given the opportunity to provide further evidence to support their estimates.  Where necessary, we will aim to contact schools in December 2025 to request additional evidence. Following this, there will be a further moderation before final outcomes are confirmed in January 2026.  Pupil number adjustments  Inaccurate estimates can cause significant adjustments to funding allocations. Each year we reconcile estimates with actual pupil numbers, through the pupil number adjustments (PNA) process.You can find more information in our academies funded on estimates guidance.   Where this adjustment is positive (for instance, where funded numbers are less than those recorded in the census) it is made in the same academic year. Where funded numbers are higher than those recorded in the census, we will recover funds in the 2027 to 2028 academic year.  Trusts that have met the conditions in their funding agreement to move to census funding  It may be that the basis of funding for your academy will change to the census for 2026 to 2027. This is because, for most estimate funded institutions, determining the pupil number basis for funding requires certain conditions to be met. These conditions are set out in the funding agreement and, in most cases, relate to occupancy levels and/or that there are pupils in all expected year groups. We will use the autumn 2025 census to establish if these conditions have been met.  If the conditions have been met, the academy will move onto the...

taxons.title: School and academy financial management and assurance
